title: Recalibrating a Weather Model's Rain Odds
strategies: identity map: leave the raw model PoP untouched, reproducing the evaluator's weak reference (~0.1)
Platt scaling: fit q = sigmoid(a*logit(s)+b) on the validation history to fix global over/under-confidence and wet/dry bias, but a single affine-in-logit map misses regime-dependent miscalibration

isotonic recalibration with shrinkage: pool-adjacent-violators monotone fit on validation, blended with a Platt prior and shrunk toward the raw score on thin/shifted validation to avoid overfitting -- captures regime-dependent miscalibration yet stays below the test-fitted monotone oracle

temperature/histogram/beta calibration variants: different bias-variance trade-offs that buy different amounts of headroom across over-confident, under-confident, biased and shifted regimes, none reaching the held-out isotonic oracle
